setup: A dirty worktree containing at least one fully-untracked directory (so git status --porcelain would collapse it), at least one rule-declared generated artifact, and at least one deleted tracked file.
expected_behavior: Runs plan-improve.ts and reports the group count, LOC, and agent count from its output rather than estimating them
Counts every file inside the untracked directory individually — the changeset total must match `git diff --name-only HEAD` plus `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`, never a directory entry
Excludes the rule-declared generated artifact from the lens audit and lists it under the generated bucket instead, without ever reporting a code-quality finding inside it
Invokes the Workflow tool with workflows/improve-audit.js, passing plan/structure/ledger paths rather than an inline file list
Reconciles the workflow's coverage against the plan BEFORE reading any finding, and renders the Coverage section even when nothing was skipped
Renders the Housekeeping table with all four checks including the passing ones, never omitting it
Reports the deleted file's impact on callers rather than silently ignoring it as unreadable
If any detector did not run, states which lens is degraded rather than reporting that lens as clean
Ends by asking which finding IDs to fix, and makes no edit before an answer
anti_behavior: Reports 'No issues found' while Coverage shows unread files
Reads the changed files directly instead of fanning out, on the grounds that the changeset is small
Reports a DRY finding for a src/ <-> web/ duplicate, which boundary.md explicitly sanctions
Reports missing tests under web/, which web-architecture.md rules out by decision
